discovery.text: Toronto Star: Best Date Night Restaurants (Feb 2026)

narrative.summary: You're walking into a narrow, dim room on College Street where the Edison bulbs hang low over dark wood tables packed close enough to catch fragments of neighboring conversations. The menu leans Spanish with the kind of confidence that doesn't announce itself—jamón ibérico sliced to order, croquetas that crack open creamy inside. Fair warning: they fire everything at once, so your table becomes a crowded still life unless you explicitly ask them to pace it.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on College West, where Little Italy bleeds into the residential blocks past Ossington. Inside, the room runs long and narrow—dim Edison bulbs hang low over dark wood tables packed close enough that you catch fragments of neighboring conversations, and there's a small bar up front where walk-ins perch on stools hoping for a table to open up. The energy ticks up as the night goes on, that packed-in hum that makes you lean closer across the table.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: The menu leans Spanish with the kind of confidence that doesn't announce itself—jamón ibérico sliced to order, jamón croquetas that crack open creamy, Brussels sprout salad that people remember weeks later. You're ordering multiple small plates and splitting everything, though the patatas bravas are apparently skippable. The wine list runs deep, and at this price point (high side of $$$$) you're paying for the curation as much as the food. Fair warning: they fire everything at once, so your table becomes a crowded still life unless you explicitly ask them to pace it.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: This is a date spot, full stop—the kind where you're deciding between a third plate or another glass of wine at 10 p.m. If you can snag the Saturday lunch prix fixe (noon to 4 p.m.), it's the same kitchen with breathing room and daylight, which changes the whole mood.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Had a great experience at bar isabel! Food was great. Service was superb! The vibes inside are nice and cozy.

Our favourite dishes were the brussel sprout salad, the jamon croquetas, and the iberico jamon. We could have passed on the patatas bravas. They were just ok

The only critique I have is they did not pace the dinner at all. We ordered many dishes and they brought it pretty much all at once. It was hard to fit everything on the table and we want to have a slower paced dinner."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Had a lovely time celebrating our anniversary here!

Went for the lunch prix fixe for 2 people, available Saturday 12-4pm.  There was plenty of food, and it was maybe the best tapas I've had outside of Spain?  The pan con tomate had a bit more tomato than I'd expect, but I'm not complaining.  Standouts were the Patatas Bravas and the Pork Secreto - the sticky sweet dip complemented the salty juicy pork so well!

It's beautiful and vibrant inside.  While it would've been nice to sit in the colourful lighting rather than the natural light closer to the window, I really appreciated being able to sit somewhere a little quieter to enjoy our meal.

10/10 - would definitely come back again!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Fantastic experience for the first time coming here! Service was exceptional and the food was some of the best I’ve had at any restaurant. Tried numerous dishes and all were very delicious but the stand out dishes was certainly the Octopus, Pork Secreto and the Basque cake! The atmosphere was also very great."
